mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during periods of rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with increasing housing starts reflects synchronized consumer demand across goods and housing, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with mean forward return <= 0.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds a 3.0 percent annual threshold, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate sales, directly translating to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth rates correspond to negative mean forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain disruptions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO shocks fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with positive outcomes occurring in > 60% of triggered instances.
